generic_rules: Focus repairs on resolving AGREE syntax and type errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize fixing unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations when AGREE reports type errors.
Ensure AGREE contracts use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `int`, `real`, and `bool` for AGREE scalar type declarations.
Ensure helper declarations match the expected expression types (e.g., comparison expressions are `bool`).
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing contract syntax or references.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model.
Do not attempt to repair contracts referencing undefined identifiers.
